strategy: one-shot: all 24 questions in a single API call per persona

questions.text: Can you describe how you usually organized your study routine during the course?
Did you tend to study at regular times each week, or did your pattern vary depending on deadlines or other demands?
What usually prompted you to log into the LMS?
Were there moments when using the LMS became part of your normal routine? If so, how did that happen?
Which LMS activities or resources felt most useful to your learning? Why?
Were there resources you often ignored, delayed, or used only minimally? Why?
What made an activity feel worth your time?
Did optional activities feel different from required ones? In what way?
Did you ever study in ways that were not visible in the LMS, such as using downloaded files, notes, textbooks, or external resources?
Can you describe situations in which you were learning or studying, but your LMS activity would not show much evidence of that?
Did you ever access materials once and then continue studying them offline?
Were there moments when it became harder to engage with the course? What was happening at that time?
Did workload, confusion, fatigue, or competing responsibilities affect how you used the LMS?
Were there times when you accessed materials but did not feel meaningfully engaged with them?
Did you ever postpone or avoid certain activities even when you knew they were important? Why?
How did deadlines influence the way you used the LMS?
Did feedback affect how or when you engaged with the course?
How did the structure of the course influence your participation?
Were there aspects of the course design that made engagement easier or harder?
Sometimes students show high activity in the LMS but submit little work. What do you think might explain that?
Sometimes students show low activity in the LMS but still perform reasonably well. Does that reflect anything from your experience?
What do you think a course log can show accurately about engagement, and what do you think it misses?
What does 'being engaged' in a course mean to you?
Is there anything important about your engagement with the course that LMS data would not show?
